fieldDescription: Add Guardrails to every message. The guardrails aim to keep the model on track and allow for content appropriate for Starsectors dystopian setting while restricting certain topics. Effectivity varies depending on the model. Enabled by default.
The max amount of tokens that are returned in an answer. A token is roughly 0.8 words. Thinking models may need considerably more tokens as the thinking process also takes from this budget.
When enabled, prompts the model to create a summary of the conversation along with a in-game timestamp. The summary is persisted.
The amount of words the model can spend on character memory.
Only affects models capable of reasoning. Common values are medium or high.

defaultValue: Default settings for newly created characters. Can be changed on a per-character basis. Character settings persist across saves and [take precedence] over the defaults listed here.

fieldDescription: A lower temperature makes it more likely that the conversation stays on track but might get repetitive, a higher temperature allows for a more diverse output that's prone to incoherence.
Limits the pool of words in a conversation to the K highest candidates.
Limits the pool of words in a conversation to those of whose probabilities sum up to P.

defaultValue: Tool Calls allow the model to directly access Starsector via a set of pre-defined tools during conversations. [This may happen without player consent] - 9 out of 10 REDACTED recommend turning this setting on.

fieldDescription: Shows a notification whenever the model requests a tool call.
An authentification token provided by a service. Usually required.

defaultValue: These settings only affect the Combat Chatter integration.

fieldDescription: An authentification token provided by a service. Usually required.
